item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
this is stupid.

(-1)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is great.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I felt very honored to have this read.

Also honored by whoever decided to animate 366-Tutella with (I think?) Touhou characters. (At about 12 min 50 sec in).

And by Traffic Resumes. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Came here because of Dr. Cimmerian's read and I'm so glad I did. This was excellently absurd.

Immediate headcanon, because I love smooshing things together, is that this has something to do with Directorate K.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It gradually becomes weirder and weirder, until:

Engage traveler with sidearm

The transportation system will distend into a helix and engulf any injured or terminated travelers. Traffic resumes.

Upvoting resumes.

Congrats on 300, because wtf did I read and why did I enjoy it that much?

Now my tongue feels weird.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I can imagine the first contact must've been a complete disaster. I mean, they'd take it as a literal GoI invasion! And somehow, every foundation site is getting attacked! Every personnel they get is being anomalously kidnapped and who knows what fate worse than death has happened to them?! I can only imagine how far it could've escalated until one person finally did a good enough border duty job to come back and report on it to the foundation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Killed it

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is one of those beautiful SCPs that are just outlandlishly random yet specific, like Ronald Regan [sic] Cut Up While Talking. Two fingers insistently pointing at upvote. the guy in the picture has slender sexy fingers btw

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A well made WTF-SCP!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I burst out laughing at 'engage traveler with sidearm.' Not sure why …

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What the heck did I just read?
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Apparently, in order to completely disturb me, you must be incredibly random, because wow. this is one of the few times that I just had to put my phone down and take a breather. It isn't particularly scary, per se, just freaky. Completely out of this world, mind boggling freaky. Good.

item.title: Wat
item.content:encoded:
MIN CEN NO COMPEHNDS!
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That was my favorite part xD Just thinking about someone smiling and then just being gunned down is a little funny in this case, just because of the context. "Traveler Smiles. Engage with sidearm." xD its just so sudden

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
How incredibly interesting. I want this to be visualized, it's a really great concept.

+1, if possible I'd upvote it twice

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "Engage with sidearm…" LOL

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think what I like so much about this article is that to them, it's not complete random weirdness. The universe obviously operates on logic that makes perfect sense to everyone involved, except for the poor security guards.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Super weird, super interesting. Whole-hearted +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It is so good. So good!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is crazy in the best way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh man, I love this. Weird stuff, interesting…and then that last column. Damn, that came as a shock. Great SCP. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yay, I'm glad you liked it!

To the points!

+ show block
– hide block

1) Hmm. I tried to specify the toll-booth in both. I'm open to change, but I'm not sure how I would reorder it.
2) I kind of intentionally left the travelers undesignated. With -1, they can capture and examine them at a fair level of detail before they get mobbed at multiple sites and have to release them. The travelers are never seen outside of -2, so Foundation is very limited in their ability to study them (due to the interference of angry -1s) and capture/recovery is impossible. They could be more -1s/humans, but they could also be something completely else, like perfectly-externally-anthropomorphic dogs or unintelligent extensions of the tongue.

It's also why they haven't separately classified the transportation system. There just aren't enough data.

Bonus material for point two (spoilers? Probably not.):

+ show block
– hide block

There are several ways you could take the shortness of the procedure list in 366-Tutella. One is that it's so short because they've only managed to figure out a few behaviours, perhaps the most common, and they're still trying to figure out countless others.

Another possibility is that that list is completely comprehensive, and that those actions are all that the travelers ever do.

Option 1 might lean towards -1s/dog-men/what have you, while option 2 might lean towards weird tongue polyps/ventriloquist dummies/those metal things from carnival shooting ranges.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No problem! It's one of my favorites.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you for pointing out SCP-2120 to me, Petrograd!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This might be my favorite contest entry. It conveys a lot within the confines of the contest, while still being ambiguous, and completely weird.

I think the description would be more clear after a few changes:
1. The description of -2 says that Foundation agents sit "in the toll-booth," although Tutella seems to imply they are sitting in the tongue.
2. I think it is implied that "travelers" are -1 instances, although that is not stated.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That's fair. I wasn't really trying to make this weird for the sake of weirdness, at least in that almost all of the content was based on creative decisions I had *some* logic for. (Also, I wasn't trying to preempt criticism with the sense comment, I just enjoy the idea of people who don't quite work by the same rules.)

That said, the narrative doesn't really go beyond, "People from elsewhere are kidnapping Foundation personnel to do a job they find tedious, and we can't seem to stop them."

I know that's pretty skimpy, but that's as far as I could take it without really diving deep into the motivations of the otherworld folks, and I wasn't quite ready for that. Still, fair point.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is just too much random for me. I see I've been name-checked upthread, and no, not everything has to make sense. The writer, in portraying those kinds of things, though, still has the responsibility to produce some sort of narrative. And in the sense that this is a narrative of the Foundation documenting something weird, and wow it's weird, yes, it is that.

People are responding well to the imagery. That's fine. I'm not against this surviving, not that there's any doubt of that happening. But the lack of traditional structure in storytelling means that this comes down to personal reaction, even more so than usual, and in that sense I can't say I responded to it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have to say that the last bit caught me off guard in a good way. Now I'm curious as to what a smiling traveler will do if they aren't terminated. I'm guessing they're some sort of aberrant that's considered to have some kind of disease, or they're extremely dangerous. Not sure which, and I hope I don't find out :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This may now be my favorite thing in the contest. I just love me some well-done wtfness.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I am beyond pleased that you guys are enjoying this so much. This is now my highest upvoted article, so no matter how the contest goes I am honored.

Things!
– hide block

I was kind of concerned that it was unjustified weirdness, but you managed to explain it all in one sentence, so kudos for that as well.

Yay, thanks! That was sort of the germ of the idea.

I think whoever gets stuck with laminated IDs should be issued a laxative (not insisting due to space limit).

They're not that filling. :P Still, that's not an unreasonable request. 366-Tutella personnel get a lot of perks in exchange for the level of craziness they're expected to put up with.

Alternatively, if you're worried about disposing of potentially anomalous material… gross.

I'd love to see how they found all of these procedures out.

You get a lot of opportunities for practice when they just keep kidnapping your guards.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fucking beautiful. I'd love to see how they found all of these procedures out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is utter madness. +1.

Under 'possible inspirations', I was seriously expecting: 'Damn, these endless queues at toll booths in rush hour are such a drag, how can I pass the time????'

May I upvote again? And again? And yet again?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Third this! It's great.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Traveler smiles. Engage traveler with sidearm.

Aaaand that's +1. I think whoever gets stuck with laminated IDs should be issued a laxative (not insisting due to space limit).

Fantastic. As you said, alternate realities don't have to make sense. This is a beautiful encapsulation of WTF.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I fucking love this, I love everything about it. It's like the Giant's Arm with more Papers, Please! and tongues and people pretending they're tacticool militarimen. +1 and how!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah. This one is pretty goddamn spectacular. I was kind of concerned that it was unjustified weirdness, but you managed to explain it all in one sentence, so kudos for that as well.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I absolutely love this, and of all the contest submissions I've read so far it is by far the best. Congratulations for being one of the few to truly grasp what this contest was meant to be.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is 100% my kind of strange. I had to read through it twice. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Quite like this. Enthusiastically upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
And now my mind is full of WTF.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's weird as fuck and I love it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Second this. Wacky and truly bizarre!

Have my +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This might just be one of my new favorites in general.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So, a Bizarro-world edition of Papers, Please?

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What if they're in another country (say, the other side of the planet)? How does that really work?

World maps. Plus, most versions of the Foundations are world organizations and have been so for a while. A few headcanons give them a national origin, but they generally quickly spilled over.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So wonderfully bizarre. I love it. I think the best part is the fact that if the guy starts peeling off his own skin you just give him the "stop fucking around and get to work" glare but if the guy just smiles you blast his brains out. It definitely gives off that vibe that Kalinin described as operating under a logic that we just can't understand.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ show block
– hide block

What if they're in another country (say, the other side of the planet)? How does that really work?

Still works. 2771-1s can go anywhere probably just as easily as anywhere else.

As for the mechanism, they don't speak English (or any language really), but they are highly fluent in frustrating bureaucratic horseshit. Edit: so the Foundation is speaking their language.

I was a bit worried about 2771-2. I'll see if I can give it its own sentence without breaking the wordcount.

Edit made! We're now at 500 words even. Sheesh.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ show block
– hide block

Should SCP-2771-1 appear at any other Foundation facility, security personnel are to point insistently to the location of Area-2771 on a map until they disperse.

What if they're in another country (say, the other side of the planet)? How does that really work?

Pros:

  • It's quirky weird that I am simply stupefied by it.
  • While silly and impractical, the procedures are more or less unique and got me chuckling.

Cons:

  • I was slightly lost midway into the Description (the part about 2771-2), but at least the ending brought me back.

Verdict: Upvote (tentatively)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wh… what… what the fuck.

Really though, +1 for the appropriate response to someone peeling the skin off their face being "hey, could we please keep going?" That was hilarious and kinda creepy at the same time and really made the article for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Here's my shot for the contest; the count for me was 499 500 words. Thanks to Agent MacLeod and TwistedGears in chat and psul and Eta Carinae on the forums for feedback and formatting help.

Image is mine.

So, just for fun:

Thesis Statement (Spoilers?)
– hide block

"This job is so easy we could give it to those weird people who use language."


Possible inspirations... (More spoilers?)
– hide block

- Outsourcing
- People playing Pictionary
- The Disney Point
- Peter Pan
- Videos of people screwing with cops at checkpoints

Honorable mention to djkaktus for demonstrating that alternate dimensions/timelines/what-have-you don't have to make sense.
